web_results.description: Feroli's prediction goes against the expectation of financial market participants, who are pricing in a likelihood of two quarter-point cuts in the Fed's benchmark rate in 2026, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool, which forecasts rate ...

web_results.title: Fed Outlook 2026: Rate Forecasts and Fixed Income Strategies | iShares
web_results.description: ... At its final meeting of 2025, the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points to a range of 3.50% to 3.75%; the Fed has now cut rates by 175 basis points since September 2024.2

web_results.title: What’s Next for the Fed in 2026? | Morningstar
web_results.description: Morningstar senior US economist Preston Caldwell is also expecting two rate cuts in 2026, one in the first half and another in the second. He says those cuts could happen slower than the pace the market is currently pricing for, since inflation ...

web_results.title: The Outlook for Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 | Goldman Sachs
web_results.description: Goldman Sachs Research forecasts that US economic growth will accelerate to 2-2.5% in 2026 because of reduced impact from tariffs as well as tax cuts and easier financial conditions. Our economists expect these factors to boost job creation and stabilize the unemployment rate at a level only modestly above the 4.4% recorded in September this year. Hatzius expects the Fed to pause its cutting cycle in January before delivering cuts in March and June, pushing the funds rate down to a terminal level of 3-3.25% (compared with 3.75%-4% currently).
